HARD GUARDRAILS (violating any = failure):
- EDIT ONLY the champion repo /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch gate-s0-s5). NEVER edit /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (read-only reference).
- FAITHFULNESS FROZEN: never modify src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py or any strict_verify / citation-verification logic. READ ONLY. No new verification pass.
- Every new behavior behind a default-OFF flag (or a None-default kwarg) so the OFF path is BYTE-IDENTICAL to today's champion. Follow the repo's existing flag pattern.
- Additive & minimal. Prefer NEW modules over rewriting. Never invent a hard constraint that isn't from explicit prompt text (design rule).
- Scope must reach RETRIEVAL query generation вҖ” NEVER a post-fetch filter of a frozen corpus (the 997->131 anti-pattern).
- Do NOT run any full ~35-min live compose or RACE/FACT scoring (a workflow shell caps at 10 min). Use fast unit tests, OFFLINE compiles, BANKED replays, and query-gen TELEMETRY (no fetch) only. If a step needs a live run, BUILD + unit-test it and leave a documented harness; do not execute it.
- If a task needs a frozen/forbidden file, STOP and report instead.
SPEC DOCS (read both FIRST, in full):
- Consolidated design + build sequence: /home/polaris/polaris_project/GATE_DESIGN_CONSOLIDATED.md
- Sol's detailed hook map + schema: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/reviews/gate_gate_design.md
- (fallback if that path 404s) /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/reviews/sol_gate_design.md

IMPLEMENT S5-HARNESS вҖ” build (do NOT run) the end-to-end fresh autonomous run + score harness.
- A script scripts/run_gate_e2e.py that, given a DRB task id, runs the FULL fresh pipeline with PG_GATE=1 autonomous (gate -> FS-Researcher fresh retrieval -> outline FEED -> compose -> render -> compliance audit) and writes report.md + planning_gate_artifact.json + contract_compliance.json + telemetry. It should also support scoring via the existing scripts/score_report_race.py and the FACT utils pipeline, and support N draws.
- A DRY SMOKE only: run the harness in a --dry mode that exercises the gate + planning + wiring WITHOUT live retrieval/compose (mock or --plan-only), proving the harness assembles the full pipeline call for tasks {4,30,61,72,76,90}. Do NOT execute a real compose (>10 min / costs money).
- Document at the top of the script the EXACT commands to run the real S5 (fresh e2e + RACE + FACT, 3 draws) so the main session can execute+monitor them.
Report the harness path, the dry-smoke output, and the documented real-run commands.

- deep-research: Deep research harness вҖ” fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly вҖ” if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highвҶ’max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only вҖ” it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK вҖ” model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER вҖ” read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" вҖ” whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) вҖ” never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# The Research Planning Gate вҖ” Consolidated Design (Opus)
2	
3	**Consolidates two independent designs** (Sol / GPT-5.6 code-grounded, Fable 5) + a 2026 best-practices digest.
4	**Date:** 2026-07-16 В· **Status:** DESIGN вҖ” build only after operator approval; build runs in a workflow, main session monitors.
5	**Target repo:** `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` (champion `bot/outline-agent-box @ df4118a`). Never edit `flywheel/`.
6	
7	> **The thesis both designers reached independently:** the gate produces ONE durable, typed **research contract**, then COMPILES it into levers the champion pipeline *already exposes but the driver never populates* вҖ” retrieval-backend routing (`ResearchFrame`), FS-Researcher query text, and `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec`. **Scope always ADDS/ROUTES queries at retrieval; it NEVER filters a frozen corpus.** A disclosure-only compliance audit reads the same contract at the end. Faithfulness (`strict_verify`) is untouched.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1. Non-negotiables (carried from operator rulings)
12	1. **FAITHFULNESS FROZEN.** No edit to `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py` / `strict_verify` / the drop rule. No new verification pass. A conformance test asserts a clean diff and that every recompose path still traverses the unchanged verifier.
13	2. **No starvation.** Scope reaches FS-Researcher *query generation* and backend routing BEFORE the first fetch. Any post-fetch filter is a secondary safety net for explicit prohibitions only вҖ” never the sole scope mechanism. (The 997вҶ’131 filter that tanked RACE 0.4447вҶ’0.3264 is the banned anti-pattern.)
14	3. **Never invent a constraint.** Enforced by TYPE: a hard/binding term requires `origin == explicit` (a real prompt span) or an affirmative user action. Inferred/default terms may only weight, prefer, or route вҖ” never hard-gate, exclude, or become a stated requirement.
15	4. **Priority COVERAGE > INSIGHT > READABILITY.** Length is planning context, never a truncation gate. Required coverage drives the gap ledger. Tone is prose-only.
16	5. **Fail-open.** Any gate error вҶ’ empty/degraded contract вҶ’ every lever reads its current default вҶ’ byte-identical to today's champion. The gate can only ADD guardrails; it can never abort a run or drop a source.
17	6. **Reuse, don't rebuild.** Reuse the validated rule-reader (`round1-if-compiler:constraint_extractor.py`), the intake extractors, FS-Researcher, the ResearchFrame router, `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec`, the outline agent. New code is small and additive.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 2. Architecture (both modes)
22	
23	```
24	caller passes mode EXPLICITLY (interactive | autonomous)   вҶҗ Sol: never infer mode from terminal/timeout
25	        в”Ӯ   benchmark driver ALWAYS passes autonomous
26	        в–ј
27	[1 UNDERSTAND]  deterministic candidate extraction (rule-reader + intake extractors, exact spans)
28	        в–ј
29	[2 COMPILE]     one structured LLM call вҶ’ typed ResearchContract (every clause accounted for;
30	                each term tagged explicit/inferred/user + force hard/prefer/open)
31	        в–ј
32	[3 VALIDATE+TRIAGE]  deterministic: span/quote equality, no inferred-hard, clause coverage, conflicts
33	        в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
34	   INTERACTIVE                          AUTONOMOUS  (benchmark)
35	   show editable plan + diff            ask nobody, wait for nobody
36	   ask 0вҖ“3 MATERIAL questions           least-restrictive open defaults
37	   edit / answer / approve  в”Җв”Җв”җ         disclose assumptions; auto-pin      вҶҗ the two modes differ at ONLY this node
38	        в–ј                     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
39	[4 PLAN+PIN]    2nd structured call вҶ’ threads, query intents, outline seed, coverage matrix; hash+pin (immutable)
40	        в–ј
41	[5 PROJECTIONS] same pinned artifact вҶ’ retrieval | outline | compose | render   (typed per-stage views)
42	        в–ј
43	[6 COMPLIANCE AUDIT]  term-level SATISFIED/FAILED/UNSATISFIABLE/UNKNOWN, routed to owning stage
44	                      (disclosure-only; strict_verify untouched)
45	```
46	
47	- **Interactive** (real users): вүӨ3 material questions; editable plan (Gemini-style natural-language edits); retrieval waits for approve/edit/answer/"use best judgment"; never crosses into autonomous on a timeout.
48	- **Autonomous** (benchmark; `PG_GATE_AUTONOMOUS=1`, the default in `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py`): a pure `contractвҶ’contract` function, **no I/O, never blocks**. Every would-ask becomes an open-ended assumption surfaced in report front-matter. *Enforced by a test asserting zero `needs_input` exits with no input channel.* This is the load-bearing mode вҖ” without it the gate can't be RACE-scored.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 3. The contract schema (merged)
53	Adopt **Sol's typed governance skeleton** (it's the more complete one) with **Fable's `Tagged`-provenance discipline** on every term:
54	
55	- **Envelope:** immutable `PlanningGateArtifact` вҖ” `mode`, `state`, `original_prompt`, `contract`, `plan`, `clarification_questions`, `revisions` (JSON-Patch), `contract_sha256`/`plan_sha256`/`artifact_sha256`. Reuse the existing `research_planner.py:serialize_plan_canonical`/`plan_sha256`.
56	- **Every term = `ContractTerm{value, origin(explicit|user|inferred|policy_default), force(hard|prefer|open), spans[], rationale, enforcement_stages[]}`.** `explicit` requires exact `prompt[start:end]` quote equality. **Hard force is invalid unless origin==explicit/user** (the mechanical no-invention guarantee).
57	- **Groups:** `ObjectiveSpec` (question, purpose, audience, output_language, depth), `ScopeSpec` (source_types, quality, languages, date, geography/jurisdiction, domains, named_sources, prohibited), `ContentSpec` (required/optional `CoverageRequirement`s incl. comparisons/metrics/counterevidence + entities + breadth/depth), `DeliverableSpec` (kind, sections w/ exact-title & order locks, length, visuals, citation, rhetoric/tone), plus `ambiguities[]`, `assumptions[]`, `conflicts[]`, `complexity`.
58	- **Plan (separate, adapts to evidence while contract stays pinned):** `threads`, `evidence_needs`, `query_intents` (each carrying its scope term-ids + language/date/source lanes), `outline_seed`, **`coverage_matrix`** (every binding term вҶ’ owning stage(s) + вүҘ1 retrieval lane), `budget` (`mandatory_lane_count`, overflow = expand-or-fail, **never silently truncate a mandatory lane**), `stop_conditions` (semantic sufficiency, not a source-count quota).
59	
60	---
61	
62	## 4. The understanding engine
63	A **bounded hybrid**, not one blind call (both agree; digest confirms):
64	1. **Deterministic candidate pass** вҖ” reuse `round1-if-compiler` `constraint_extractor` вҠ• champion `intake_constraint_extractor` (`extract_constraints_regex`, `extract_instruction_slots`, `extract_scope_constraints`). Preserve exact offsets. **Deterministic wins on overlap; LLM fills gaps.** One candidate adapter вҖ” one canonical contract, no competing truth sources.
65	2. **Contract-compiler call** вҖ” structured JSON; system prompt forbids marking implications as explicit, forbids inventing date/geo/source/length, requires open/null for unspecified, decomposes compound prompts preserving dependencies, records ambiguities + assumptions + conflicts, returns `clause_coverage` (every operative clause dispositioned). **Raise the current 2,000-token `plan_research` cap** вҖ” inadequate for compound contracts (Sol); use provider-resolved max + bounded 2-call structure.
66	3. **Deterministic validate + triage** вҖ” quote equality, no inferred-hard, every candidate represented-or-rejected, every clause dispositioned, conflicts surfaced. One correction retry вҶ’ else conservative span-verified fallback (raw prompt as objective, explicit rules only, open elsewhere; `compiler_degraded=true`).
67	4. **Plan-compiler call** вҖ” from the *validated contract*: smallest complete thread set (scale to complexity, no fixed quota), every evidence-bearing requirement gets вүҘ1 mandatory query intent, scope encoded in query intents (hardвҶ’query+backend filter, softвҶ’ranking), coverage matrix built, exact headings only from explicit instruction.
68	
69	Cost: 2 small-model calls (glm-5.2, same as FS query-gen) вҖ” negligible vs retrieval+outline.
70	
71	---
72	
73	## 5. Per-stage enforcement (merged hook map)
74	
75	| Stage | What the contract does | Champion hook (verified) |
76	|---|---|---|
77	| **Gate entry** | compile/load artifact, require approved/auto_pinned, persist `planning_gate_artifact.json` | new `planning/research_planning_gate.py:run_research_planning_gate`; in `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query` replace the raw `plan_research(_clean_question,вҖҰ)` decision |
78	| **Retrieval (the no-starvation core)** | `source_typesвҶ’evidence_needs` routes scholarly backends (S2/OpenAlex) = **GO FIND journals**; scope terms + languages + entities into query TEXT; hard scope вҶ’ backend-native filter, soft вҶ’ ranking | `live_retriever.py:run_live_retrieval` (accepts `research_frame`/`protocol`/`amplified_queries`); `fs_researcher_query_gen.py:_plan_expert_facet_queries` + `_multilingual_native_reserve` + `sub_entity_query_expander`; `expert_facet_planner._question_anchor`. **FS branch in `run_one_query` currently passes only `_clean_question` and discards `_research_plan.sub_queries` вҖ” fix that.** |
79	| **Outline (FEED)** | seed required sections + pre-load `required_coverage`/`must_address` as PENDING gaps so the deep-think loop CLOSES contract gaps; thread scope into gap search | `outline_agent.py:run_outline_agent_or_legacy` (already takes `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec` вҶ’ `outline_digest.build_requirements_block`). **BUG (both found): `_tool_search_more_evidence` drops `protocol`/`research_frame` вҶ’ deep-think search runs unscoped. Thread scope through `OutlineWorkspace`.** (accepts `**_ignored` today вҖ” clean seam) |
80	| **Compose** | tone/audience/pov into the section advisory-prose slot (prose only); document_type selects skeleton | `multi_section_generator.py:generate_multi_section_report` (:~3010 seams), `_call_section`, `_select_section_system_prompt`. Length = planning context, never truncation. |
81	| **Render** | required sections/order, tables from VERIFIED fields only, references dedup by work, length reported | `compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:main` assembly вҖ” thread specs; keep `_audit_citations`. Mark `retrieval_scope_status` when run on a prebuilt corpus. |
82	| **Compliance audit** | term-level satisfied/failed/unsatisfiable/unknown + owning stage; deterministic where possible, one cheap judge for semantic coverage | new `planning/contract_compliance.py:audit_contract`, run AFTER assembly ALONGSIDE (never touching) the frozen faithfulness tripwire. Disclosure-only. |
83	
84	---
85	
86	## 6. Gate вҶ” outline-agent seam: **FEED** (both agree)
87	Gate replaces the *pre-retrieval* planning call; the outline agent stays the *post-retrieval evidence-aware refiner*. Refactor `run_outline_agent_or_legacy` into `refine_outline_from_seed(seed_outline, contract, retrieval_projection, coverage_matrix, вҖҰ)` with the legacy path as `seed = supplied_seed or legacy_call_outline(...)`. Seed behavior depends on provenance: explicit/user headings = immutable title+order; gate-proposed headings = stable `section_id` + revisable title, splittable/mergeable only if every binding term stays mapped. **Required topics are coverage obligations, NOT automatically headings** (the round1 mistake). Extend `OutlineWorkspace` with the contract hash + term ledger; validate every `update_outline` against it (reject dropping an explicit lock or the last owner of a binding term). Degrades to exact champion behavior when the contract names no structure.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## 7. Ask-vs-assume (merged policy)
92	Ask **iff uncertain AND consequential**, вүӨ3, interactive only. Eligible only if: not already answered, вүҘ2 plausible interpretations remain, the choice materially changes discovery/structure/decision, and open-leaving wastes real research. Autonomous = **zero asks**, least-restrictive open defaults, every assumption disclosed. Locked defaults: no hard date cutoff ever (prefer-fresher soft, per operator); geography/jurisdiction stay open/comparative; source-type open+prefer-authoritative unless explicit; source-language вү  prompt language; tone/heading never trigger a question. Explicit "only/must/exclude/do not use" + supplied ranges/counts/headings = hard; "prefer/focus/especially" = soft. When a soft preference starves a mandatory thread вҶ’ relax + disclose; when two explicit terms conflict вҶ’ interactive asks, autonomous executes the feasible intersection and labels the rest `UNSATISFIABLE`.
93	
94	---
95	
96	## 8. Build sequence (spikes first; each gated; faithfulness frozen throughout)
97	Work on a fresh branch off `bot/outline-agent-box`. Every step additive + flag-gated (OFF = byte-identical) + measured.
98	
99	- **S0 вҖ” Port + reconcile (no behavior change).** Port `round1` rule-reader + tests into champion; put it and the intake extractors behind ONE candidate adapter. Do NOT port the compose-time `filter_eligible` wiring or the coverage-to-heading mapping. *Accept:* candidate reconciliation corpus; OFF path byte-identical.
100	- **S1 вҖ” Contract + plan schema + compiler (offline).** Schema, 2 structured calls, deterministic validators. *Accept:* compile all 100 DRB prompts in autonomous mode; human-audit a stratified sample вҖ” **no invented hard constraint, no missing explicit clause, an assumption record for every inferred term** (not merely valid JSON).
101	- **S2 вҖ” Retrieval projection (the no-starvation proof).** Thread `retrieval_plan`/`research_frame` into FS-Researcher + `run_live_retrieval`; scope into query text + backend routing. *Accept:* on a FRESH task-72 run, telemetry proves journal/English + every mandatory topic reach `_plan_expert_facet_queries` BEFORE results exist; the gate never reduces evidence count vs no-gate (the mechanized 997-row guard).
102	- **S3 вҖ” Outline FEED + gap-scope fix.** `refine_outline_from_seed`; seed gap ledger; fix `_tool_search_more_evidence` scope drop. *Accept:* replay banked corpora (tasks 30/61/76/90) вҖ” real refinement, locks + term-mappings preserved, gap queries stay in-scope, no second fresh planner.
103	- **S4 вҖ” Compose/render projections + compliance audit.** Thread specs; deterministic renderer; `audit_contract`. *Accept:* exact section order / comparison table / length range all trace to verified claims; term-level audit produced.
104	- **S5 вҖ” End-to-end fresh autonomous run** on tasks {4,30,61,72,76,90}: retrievalвҶ’outlineвҶ’composeвҶ’render, RACE + FACT, **3Г— per the measurement protocol**. *Accept:* RACE (esp. Instruction-Following) up vs champion, **FACT вүҘ 90.3% held**, `provenance_generator.py` clean diff.
105	
106	**Release gate is behavioral** (both insist): the contract must visibly alter FS discovery, survive outline refinement, constrain compose/render, and yield term-level audit results on REAL output. Green unit tests / a filtered fixed corpus / a source-count bump do NOT prove the gate fired.
107	
108	---
109	
110	## 9. Top risks (both designers) + mitigation
111	- **Hallucinated hard constraint** вҶ’ the `origin==explicit`-for-hard type rule + clause coverage + assumption list.
112	- **Re-introducing the filter trap** вҶ’ design has NO post-fetch scope filter path; mechanized "gate never lowers evidence count" test.
113	- **Scope loss in outline gap search** вҶ’ the `_tool_search_more_evidence` fix (both found this bug).
114	- **Two-planner fight** вҶ’ FEED + required-title subordination already in `apply_revision_ops`.
115	- **Budget truncating a mandatory lane** вҶ’ compute `mandatory_lane_count` first; expand or fail loudly.
116	- **Compliance-judge circularity** вҶ’ deterministic checks for counts/headings/length/tables/citations; a separately-configured judge only for semantic coverage; it can NEVER alter faithfulness verdicts.
117	- **Autonomous blocking** вҶ’ STAGE-2 autonomous is pure `contractвҶ’contract`; test asserts zero `needs_input` with no input channel.
118	
119	---
120	
121	*Full independent designs preserved at:* `scratchpad/reviews/sol_gate_design.md` (Sol) and the Fable agent transcript. *Best-practices digest:* `scratchpad/reviews/gate_bestpractices_digest.md`.

2	
3	The Research Planning Gate is a pre-retrieval compiler, not another report writer and not a filter over whatever evidence happens to have been retrieved. Its unit of work is a versioned, immutable `PlanningGateArtifact`: the original request, a typed research contract, a research plan, every explicit assumption or unresolved ambiguity, and stage-specific projections. All expensive stages consume the same pinned artifact and report compliance against its stable term IDs.

59	
60	The stages are deliberately split as follows.
61	
62	**Admission and mode selection.** The caller must pass `mode: "interactive" | "autonomous"`; the gate must not infer mode from a terminal, timeout, environment accident, or presence of a UI. The benchmark driver always passes `autonomous`. A product UI normally passes `interactive`. This prevents the benchmark from blocking forever on a clarification that no human can answer, and prevents an interactive request from silently switching to assumptions because a timer expired.
63	
64	**Candidate extraction.** Existing deterministic readers identify candidate constraints and their exact prompt spans before an LLM interprets the request. Port the validated `round1-if-compiler:src/polaris_graph/instruction/constraint_extractor.py` implementation and its tests into the champion, then reconcile it behind one adapter with `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/intake_constraint_extractor.py:extract_user_constraints`, `extract_instruction_slots`, and `extract_scope_constraints`. This is reuse, not a second independent truth source: candidates flow into the compiler and validators; the pinned contract is the sole downstream authority. The rejected branch behaviorвҖ”filtering a prebuilt corpus at compose timeвҖ”is not ported.
65	
66	**Contract compilation.** A structured reasoning call turns candidates and the raw request into a normalized contract. It must account for every clause, including nested questions, parentheticals, format requests, source restrictions, and exclusions. Each contract term carries origin and evidence. An item may be semantically inferred as a useful research direction, but an inferred item may never become a hard retrieval exclusion or an invented user requirement.
67	
68	**Validation and triage.** Deterministic validators reject unsupported `EXPLICIT` labels, inferred hard constraints, missing required-clause mappings, contradictory locks, invalid date ranges, and orphaned requirements. A bounded correction call receives machine-readable errors. Ambiguities are represented rather than erased.
69	
70	**Interactive branch.** The user sees the draft contract and plan as an editable artifact, plus a compact diff between their text and the normalized interpretation. The gate asks zero to three questions only when an answer can materially change retrieval or the deliverable. Retrieval does not begin until the user approves, edits, or explicitly selects вҖңuse best judgment.вҖқ Edits are recompiled, validated, and shown as a revision; approval pins the exact revision and hash.
71	
72	**Autonomous branch.** This is the mandatory benchmark path. It never emits a blocking question and never waits for approval. It resolves material ambiguity with the least restrictive defensible interpretation, keeps unspecified dimensions open, records every assumption, and auto-pins the revision under a named policy version. вҖңNo date statedвҖқ therefore means an open date range with a soft preference for fresher evidence where relevance is equalвҖ”not a fabricated five-year cutoff. An unresolvable contradiction is recorded as such; the system either continues on the non-conflicting subset with an explicit limitation or returns `UNSATISFIABLE` when the requested result is logically impossible. It must not guess a jurisdiction, population, source type, date window, or exact length simply to fill a schema field.
73	
74	**Plan compilation and pinning.** A second structured call decomposes the validated contract into research threads, query intents, and an outline seed. A deterministic compiler creates a coverage matrix from every binding contract term to at least one owning stage and, where evidence is required, at least one retrieval lane. The plan scales to the request: four to six directions are a useful common shape, not a fixed quota. Compound prompts may need more; narrow prompts may need fewer. Query caps may not silently erase mandatory threads. The canonical JSON and SHA-256 approach already present in `src/polaris_graph/planning/research_planner.py:serialize_plan_canonical` and `plan_sha256` should be retained for the richer artifact.
75	
76	**Execution and revision.** The pinned artifact is immutable. Evidence-aware outline refinement may update an execution plan, but it cannot mutate the contract. A legitimate later user change creates a new artifact revision, records a JSON Patch and reason, supersedes the old hash, and invalidates affected downstream checkpoints. Autonomous gap discovery can revise inferred query tactics without changing explicit terms; those tactical revisions are also logged.
77	
78	**Compliance.** Checkpoints audit the contract after retrieval, outline, composition, and render. A final audit reports each term as `SATISFIED`, `FAILED`, `UNSATISFIABLE`, `UNKNOWN`, or `NOT_APPLICABLE`, with output/evidence spans and the responsible stage. Compliance is distinct from factual faithfulness. `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:strict_verify` and the surrounding provenance engine are frozen: no gate change, remediation rule, or format transformation may weaken, bypass, or edit them.
79	
80	Within the space of faithful outputs, execution preserves the champion objective ordering: coverage before additional insight, and additional insight before readability polish. That ordering never licenses an unsupported claim; frozen faithfulness is an invariant, not a tradeable score.
81	
82	The primary paid-run integration point is `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query`, before either standard or FS retrieval. `scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:main` starts from a frozen corpus; it can load or autonomously compile a gate artifact to exercise outline/compose/render behavior, but it must label retrieval-scope enforcement as unavailable. Wiring only that script would repeat the known starvation error because no gate scope could influence discovery.
83	
84	## 2. The contract schema (full, typed).
85

407	## 3. The understanding engine (decomposition/clarification/inference; prompt + output schema).
408	
409	The understanding engine is a bounded hybrid pipeline rather than a single blind planner call.
410	
411	**Candidate pass.** Run the ported validated rule-reader and the championвҖҷs three intake extractors. Preserve exact character offsets. Normalize aliases (for example, вҖңacademic papersвҖқ versus вҖңjournal articlesвҖқ) without changing force. Extract verbs and coordination structure so вҖңcompare A and B on X and Y, then recommendвҖқ becomes comparison requirements for both entities and both dimensions plus a synthesis requirementвҖ”not a bag of keywords. Detect requested output objects such as a top-ten table, mind map, legal case analysis, forecast, or literature review. Candidate extraction is high-recall; it does not itself pin a contract.
412	
413	**Contract compiler call.** Use the configured planner role through the repositoryвҖҷs model resolver, maximum supported reasoning effort, and the serving providerвҖҷs real maximum completion cap. The current `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query` bridge to `plan_research` caps output at 2,000 tokens; that cap is inadequate for arbitrary compound contracts. Control cost with two bounded structured calls and strict schemas, not by starving reasoning or output.
414	
415	Recommended compiler system prompt:
416	
417	```text
418	You are the POLARIS Research Contract Compiler. Convert the user's complete request
419	into the supplied ResearchContract JSON schema. Your output is a contract for a
420	research system, not the answer to the research question.
421	
422	Rules:
423	1. Account for every imperative, question, entity, comparison dimension, scope phrase,
424	   source rule, exclusion, format request, and rhetorical instruction in the request.
425	2. A term marked origin="explicit" MUST include exact character spans copied from the
426	   request. Do not mark implications or world knowledge as explicit.
427	3. Never invent a restriction. If date, geography, jurisdiction, source type, source
428	   language, length, audience, or format is unspecified, represent it as open/null.
429	4. Inferred and policy-default terms may only be preference/open, never hard. List every
430	   such interpretation in assumptions and explain its consequence.
431	5. Distinguish source language from output language. Distinguish required topics from
432	   required section headings. Distinguish content scope from presentation shape.
433	6. Decompose compound and nested questions without dropping qualifiers. Preserve
434	   dependencies (for example, evidence first, comparison second, recommendation last).
435	7. Identify ambiguity rather than resolving it invisibly. Mark whether it can safely
436	   remain open and which execution stages it changes.
437	8. Preserve conflicting explicit instructions in conflicts; do not silently choose one.
438	9. Do not set source-count, date-window, jurisdiction, length, or section-count quotas
439	   unless the user supplied them.
440	10. Return JSON only, conforming exactly to the schema. Do not research or answer.
441	
442	INPUT:
443	- current UTC date (context only, not a user constraint)
444	- mode
445	- exact user request
446	- deterministic candidates with spans
447	- named product defaults and their policy IDs
448	```

451

454	  "contract": { "...": "ResearchContract" },
455	  "clause_coverage": [
456	    {
457	      "span": {"start": 0, "end": 18, "quote": "..."},
458	      "term_ids": ["ct_content_001"],
459	      "disposition": "represented|non_instructional|ambiguous",
460	      "explanation": "..."
461	    }
462	  ]

465	
466	**Contract validation.** Validate the JSON schema, offsets, quote equality, enum normalization, date order, stable IDs, and force/origin invariants. Then perform semantic checks: every deterministic candidate is represented or explicitly rejected with a reason; every operative prompt clause has a disposition; each assumption points to a non-explicit term; each hard term is user-supported; required item counts are preserved; and mutually exclusive requirements are surfaced. The validator returns structured errors. One correction call receives only the original request, rejected JSON, and errors. A second invalid result falls back in autonomous mode to a conservative deterministic contract containing the full raw request as the primary objective, span-verified explicit rules only, and open scope elsewhere; it records `compiler_degraded=true`. Interactive mode shows the conservative draft and asks only if the missing interpretation is material.
467	
468	**Ambiguity triage.** Generate question candidates from `Ambiguity` records, not from a generic desire for more detail. A question is eligible only when the prompt does not already answer it, at least two plausible interpretations remain, the choice materially changes retrieval/structure/decision utility, and leaving it open has meaningful expected regret. Deduplicate coupled ambiguities into one question. In interactive mode choose at most three material questions and show their consequence. In autonomous mode generate no user-facing questions; apply the policy in section 6 and keep the assumption visible.
469	
470	**Plan compiler call.** The validated contractвҖ”not the raw prompt aloneвҖ”is the input. Recommended system prompt:
471	
472	```text
473	You are the POLARIS Research Plan Compiler. Produce ResearchExecutionPlan JSON for the
474	pinned ResearchContract. Do not answer the research question and do not change the contract.
475	
476	Rules:
477	1. Create the smallest complete set of research threads that covers every binding content
478	   requirement; scale with complexity rather than targeting a fixed number.
479	2. Preserve dependencies: descriptive/baseline evidence precedes comparison, causal or
480	   correlational analysis, forecast, and recommendation where applicable.
481	3. Give every requirement needing evidence at least one mandatory query intent. Add distinct
482	   lanes when source type, language, jurisdiction, date, named source, primary-source status,
483	   comparison side, or counterevidence requires a different discovery strategy.
484	4. Scope must shape discovery. Encode explicit hard scope in query intents and backend-filter
485	   projections; encode soft/inferred scope as ranking preferences. Never plan to satisfy scope
486	   solely by filtering a fixed corpus after retrieval.
487	5. Do not drop a mandatory lane to meet a query cap. Report mandatory_lane_count and use the
488	   budget overflow policy.
489	6. Map every contract term to its owning stage(s), research thread(s), query intent(s) when
490	   evidence is needed, outline section(s) when presentation is needed, and audit method.
491	7. Required topics are not automatically headings. Create exact heading locks only from an
492	   explicit/user-approved section instruction. Otherwise use stable semantic section IDs and
493	   revisable display titles.
494	8. Treat early outline structure as a seed for evidence-aware refinement, not a factual claim
495	   that evidence exists. Define semantic sufficiency and gap conditions without inventing source
496	   counts.
497	9. Return JSON only and conform exactly to ResearchExecutionPlan.
498	```
499	
500	**Plan validation.** Compile a bipartite coverage graph and fail the plan if any binding content term has no thread, any evidence-bearing requirement has no mandatory query intent, any explicit comparison omits a side or dimension, any required deliverable element has no stage owner, any exact section lock is absent, or any mandatory lane can be silently truncated by `max_queries`. Validate that retrieval query intents carry the term IDs for all applicable source/date/language/geography/jurisdiction constraints. One structured correction retry is sufficient; otherwise use a deterministic one-thread-per-required-clause plan rather than dropping clauses.
501	
502	**Approval presentation.** The human-facing draft is generated deterministically from the typed data, not by another summarizer that can omit terms. It shows: вҖңWhat I understand,вҖқ scope (required/preferred/open), questions to answer, proposed structure, deliverable/rhetoric, assumptions, conflicts, and a term-to-stage trace. Users can approve all, edit a field, answer a question, or use best judgment. The autonomous artifact stores the same view for forensic inspection even though no UI is shown.
503	
504	**No plan drift.** All stage prompts include the artifact hash and only their typed projection. A stage may report a proposed tactical amendment, but it cannot rewrite the canonical contract in memory. Logs and raw LLM I/O record the artifact revision and term IDs. This makes a final failure traceable to extraction, planning, retrieval, outline, compose, or render rather than to an unobservable prompt reinterpretation.

532	- `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query` may call `research_planner.py:plan_research` before retrieval, but the FS-Researcher branch calls its own planner from the raw question and does not consume those subqueries.
533	- In `multi_section_generator.py:generate_multi_section_report`, supplying `research_plan` sends execution through `_assign_evidence_to_planned_outline` and bypasses `outline_agent.py:run_outline_agent_or_legacy`.
534	- Without `research_plan`, `run_outline_agent_or_legacy` asks `_call_outline` for a fresh initial structure. The result is two potential structure planners that do not share a complete contract, plus a fast path that omits the strongest evidence-aware planner.
535	
536	The clean seam is:
537	
538	```python
539	artifact = run_research_planning_gate(prompt, mode=mode, ...)
540	retrieved = run_fs_researcher_retrieval(
541	    prompt,
542	    retrieval_plan=artifact.retrieval_projection(),
543	    ...,
544	)
545	outline = await refine_outline_from_seed(
546	    research_question=prompt,
547	    evidence=retrieved.evidence,
548	    seed_outline=artifact.plan.outline_seed,
549	    contract=artifact.contract,
550	    retrieval_projection=artifact.retrieval_projection(),
551	    coverage_matrix=artifact.plan.coverage_matrix,
552	    ...,
553	)
554	report = await generate_multi_section_report(
555	    ...,
556	    resolved_outline=outline,
557	    deliverable_spec=artifact.deliverable_projection(),
558	    scope_spec=artifact.scope_projection(),
559	)
560	```
561	
562	Refactor `outline_agent.py:run_outline_agent_or_legacy` into two logical entry points while preserving the legacy adapter:
563	
564	```python
565	async def refine_outline_from_seed(
566	    *, research_question, evidence, seed_outline,
567	    contract, retrieval_projection, coverage_matrix, ...
568	) -> OutlineAgentResult: ...
569	
570	async def run_outline_agent_or_legacy(...):
571	    seed = supplied_seed or await legacy_call_outline(...)
572	    return await refine_outline_from_seed(..., seed_outline=seed)
573	```
574	
575	The gate owns intent; the outline agent owns evidence-aware organization. Specifically:
576	
577	- The gate decides the research contract, mandatory questions, scope, explicit comparisons, requested deliverable, and initial semantic section responsibilities before retrieval.
578	- FS-Researcher decides concrete query variants and adaptive next searches within that contract.
579	- The outline agent decides how available evidence is grouped, where contradictions and synthesis belong, whether an inferred section is overloaded, and which term-specific gap searches are necessary.
580	- The section generator writes from the resolved outline and verified evidence; it does not reinterpret the global request independently.
581	
582	Seed behavior must depend on provenance. An explicit or user-approved exact heading is an immutable title and, if explicitly ordered, an immutable position. A gate-proposed heading has a stable `section_id`, purpose, and coverage mapping; its display title may be refined, and it may be split/merged only if every binding requirement remains mapped. Required topics are coverage obligations, not automatically headings. Thus the bad branch mapping of every `required_coverage` string into `required_sections` must not be repeated.
583	
584	Extend `OutlineWorkspace` with the artifact/contract hash, immutable term ledger, typed retrieval projection, and section-to-term map. Every `update_outline` operation is validated against this ledger. `apply_revision_ops(required_titles=...)` already provides a useful exact-title lock, but validation must also protect order locks and вҖңlast ownerвҖқ coverage. When a section splits, term mappings must be explicitly distributed or duplicated as appropriate; when sections merge, mappings union; a retitle never changes purpose. The outlinerвҖҷs completion checklist should cite contract term IDs and prompt spans, not only free-form aspects grounded to the raw question.
585	
586	The gap-search tool is part of the seam, not an exception. `outline_agent.py:_tool_search_more_evidence` currently creates a query from the research question and missing aspect, then calls `run_live_retrieval` without the gateвҖҷs protocol or research frame. Add `term_ids` to every gap todo and construct gap queries from the associated `QueryIntent`; pass the same scope protocol/frame and hard/soft controls used by initial retrieval. Otherwise a correctly scoped first pass can be diluted by an unconstrained outline-stage search.
587	
588	This division removes planner fights. There is one user-intent contract and one pre-retrieval plan; the later agent refines its execution against actual evidence. The final resolved outline records both its gate seed hash and its revision operations, so a forensic audit can explain every difference.
589	
590	## 6. Ask-vs-assume.
591	
592	The governing rule is: ask only when the missing choice is both uncertain and consequential; otherwise proceed with the least restrictive interpretation and expose it. Questions are not a ritual, and filling every optional field is not a goal.
593	
594	An ambiguity is eligible for a question only if all of these hold:
595	
596	- The user has not already answered it directly or by an unambiguous constraint.
597	- More than one plausible interpretation remains after deterministic extraction and contract compilation.
598	- The alternatives materially change discovery, required coverage, legal/geographic applicability, a decision/recommendation, or the shape of the requested deliverable.
599	- Leaving the dimension open is likely to waste substantial research or produce a materially less useful answer.
600	- The user can reasonably choose; it is not a factual question the research itself should resolve.
601	
602	Interactive mode asks no more than three compact questions in one turn. Coupled choices are combined. Each question states why it matters, offers concrete choices including вҖңkeep broad / use best judgment,вҖқ and shows the current default. The draft plan remains editable even if zero questions qualify. Expensive retrieval waits for approval, an edit, answers, or an explicit best-judgment choice. The gate remains in `needs_input`; it does not silently cross into autonomous mode after a timeout.
603	
604	Examples that usually justify asking interactively include an ambiguous acronym that names different research domains; an unstated jurisdiction in a request for a binding legal analysis; an ambiguous entity with different markets; or a deliverable described simultaneously as an executive memo and a full systematic review. A tone preference, an unspecified heading label, or an open source-language choice normally does not justify interruption.
605	
606	Autonomous modeвҖ”the benchmark modeвҖ”asks zero questions, returns no `needs_input` state, and waits for no human. It applies these defaults:
607	
608	| Unspecified dimension | Autonomous interpretation | Force and disclosure |
609	|---|---|---|
610	| Date/recency | No hard start date. Prefer fresher evidence when relevance/quality are otherwise comparable; retain seminal/necessary older work. For вҖңcurrent/latest,вҖқ use execution date as a search tactic and audit freshness, without inventing a user-specified cutoff. | Preference; recorded assumption when it changes tactics. |
611	| Geography/market | Open/global where sensible; use geography inherent in explicitly named entities. Do not guess a country. | Open or inferred preference, never a hard filter. |
612	| Legal jurisdiction | Keep comparative/general and clearly limit conclusions. Do not select a binding jurisdiction. | Open; material limitation. |
613	| Source type | Open across credible types appropriate to the evidence need; prefer primary/authoritative sources where available. | Policy preference, not вҖңpeer-reviewed only.вҖқ |
614	| Source language | Search multilingual sources when useful and supported; do not make the prompt language an English-only/Chinese-only source rule. | Open with tactical preferences. |
615	| Output language | Use the dominant language of the request while preserving necessary names/terms. | Reversible policy default. |
616	| Audience/tone | Informed general audience; precise, neutral, and appropriately hedged. | Reversible policy default. |
617	| Length/section count | Adaptive to the number and depth of explicit requirements. No invented word count or fixed section quota. | Open. |
618	| Citation layout | ProductвҖҷs provenance-preserving default renderer. | Named policy default; never alters evidence eligibility. |
619	| Breadth/depth | Cover every explicit question with enough depth to support the requested synthesis; add context only when it serves those questions. | Inferred planning tactic, not a new content requirement. |
620	
621	Explicit constraint language remains binding. вҖңOnly,вҖқ вҖңmust,вҖқ вҖңexclude,вҖқ вҖңdo not use,вҖқ a supplied date range, named jurisdictions, exact item counts, exact headings, or an affirmative user edit can create hard terms. Softer languageвҖ”вҖңprefer,вҖқ вҖңfocus on,вҖқ вҖңespecially,вҖқ вҖңwhere possibleвҖқвҖ”normally creates preferences unless context makes it an explicit required topic. The span and rationale make that decision auditable.
622	
623	Inferred/default choices can create query tactics and optional outline suggestions, but they cannot cause source exclusion, suppress a named topic, become a required fact, or be reported as the userвҖҷs instruction. When a preference starves a mandatory thread, relax the preference and disclose the tradeoff; never relax an explicit hard prohibition. When two explicit constraints genuinely conflict, interactive mode asks for resolution. Autonomous mode preserves both in the record, executes their feasible intersection if one exists, and labels the remainder `UNSATISFIABLE`; it does not quietly select the easier instruction.
624	
625	Questions should not ask the user to do the research. вҖңWhich jurisdiction governs your situation?вҖқ can be appropriate; вҖңWhich studies should I cite?вҖқ is not, unless the user is imposing named sources. Similarly, the gate should research whether evidence exists rather than ask whether it exists.
626	
627	Once research begins, the contract remains pinned. Interactive users can intentionally amend it, creating a new revision and invalidating affected checkpoints. The system should not interrupt mid-run merely because an inferred tactic could be improved. Autonomous execution may revise open query tactics, but any revision that would contradict or weaken an explicit term is forbidden.
628	
629	## 7. Generality + risks/failures/spikes.
630	
631	The schema is intentionally domain-neutral: evidence needs and source policies are extensible strings, while the core semanticsвҖ”origin, force, scope, coverage, deliverable, ambiguity, stage ownershipвҖ”remain fixed. It must be validated against the full DeepResearch-Bench-II prompt set, not tuned to a single journal-review task. Representative treatments include:
632	
633	| Prompt shape | Expected gate behavior |
634	|---|---|
635	| DRB-II 1, current Chinese social-strata finances | Treat вҖңcurrentвҖқ as an explicit freshness objective, China and the nine strata as explicit scope/entities, and income/financial condition/middle-class analysis as distinct requirements. Generate current-statistics and definitional/method lanes; do not invent a five-year cutoff. |
636	| DRB-II 4, gold since 2010 with mind map and forecast | Encode 2010вҶ’present as hard date scope, historical analysis/forecast/support/resistance as separate dependent requirements, and mind map as a render obligation. Forecast language must be hedged; the renderer may build the map only from verified content. |
637	| DRB-II 30, Global South academic theory | Preserve the named theoretical lensesвҖ”non-Western modernization, postcolonialism, Orientalism, and global historyвҖ”as explicit coverage terms, not optional keywords. Academic depth becomes a deliverable requirement; no invented journal-only filter follows from вҖңacademic.вҖқ |
638	| DRB-II 41, top-ten films with comparisons and inference | Preserve exact item count, all comparison dimensions, China box-office scope, and the final synthesis dependency. Create a comparison-matrix visual slot tied to verified fields for all ten items. |
639	| DRB-II 61, chub mackerel across Pacific Rim markets | Create market, interannual biological measures, price dynamics, oceanographic factors, and correlation/synthesis threads with explicit cross-market dependencies. Keep source types open; route scientific, fisheries, and market-data evidence needs separately. |
640	| DRB-II 72, English journal-only labor literature review | Compile English source language and journal-only as explicit hard retrieval rules and literature-review shape as a deliverable rule. Seed scholarly FS queries and backend filters before retrieval. This is the primary no-starvation regression; filtering a general frozen corpus is not an acceptable test. |
641	| DRB-II 76, compound health/gut request | Preserve every folded question, entities/interventions/pathogens/metabolites/diet dimensions, and their dependencies. The plan expands beyond a default direction count as needed; it does not compress the request into one broad microbiome query. |
642	| DRB-II 90, ADAS liability, technology, law, and cases | Separate technical capability, legal doctrine, cases, responsibility boundaries, and recommendations. If jurisdiction is absent, interactive mode asks because it changes legal authority; autonomous mode stays comparative/general, searches multiple relevant jurisdictions, and states the limitation rather than guessing one. |
643	| Narrow single-fact request | Produce a small contract and plan; do not manufacture a multi-section deep-research skeleton or four-to-six artificial facets. If the product still requires deep research, add source verification tactics without adding user requirements. |
644	| Wide-open exploratory request | Keep scope dimensions open, infer revisable thematic directions, show them as assumptions, and let evidence-aware outline refinement consolidate them. Do not turn exploratory examples into exclusions. |
645	| Non-English or mixed-language request | Preserve prompt spans in the original text, normalize aliases without translation loss, choose output language by policy if unstated, and create source-language lanes based on topic/geography rather than equating output and source language. |
646	
647	Risks and failure behavior are part of the design rather than deferred implementation details:
648	
649	- **Hallucinated constraints.** A compiler may turn a plausible convention into a user rule. Exact-span validation, the hard-origin invariant, clause coverage, and explicit assumption lists are the defense. Test adversarial prompts where a domain often uses a convention the user did not request.
650	- **Constraint under-extraction.** Nested clauses, negation, mixed languages, tables, and parentheticals can be missed. Run the deterministic and LLM views independently as candidate sources, then require disposition of every operative clause. Do not resolve disagreement by taking the narrower interpretation silently.
651	- **Two competing rule readers.** The branch compiler and champion intake extractors overlap. Put them behind one candidate adapter and pin one canonical contract. Downstream modules must not re-read raw text to create new hard rules.
652	- **FS plan loss.** The current FS branch ignores the existing `ResearchPlan` subqueries. A behavioral test must assert that every mandatory `QueryIntent.intent_id` appears in FS todo/query telemetry before any evidence is selected.
653	- **Budget truncation.** Fixed `max_queries`, rounds, or small completion caps can erase compound requirements. Compute mandatory lane count first; expand or fail loudly. Use parallelism for speed and provider-resolved maximum completion caps for reasoning/output safety.
654	- **Post-retrieval starvation.** A compose-time source filter can improve apparent constraint purity while destroying evidence breadth. Test hard scope with fresh discovery and verify query/backend traces. Banked-corpus replay remains useful only for downstream behavior.
655	- **Scope loss in gap retrieval.** The outline agentвҖҷs current gap tool omits protocol/frame. Tests must force an outline gap under a hard source/language/jurisdiction contract and prove that the follow-up query retains it.
656	- **Outline plan fights.** The generator currently chooses between a supplied plan and the agentic outliner. Refactor to seed-and-refine, and test that explicit locks survive while evidence-aware revisions still occur.
657	- **Coverage-to-heading overreach.** Required topics may belong together. Validate term ownership, not one-heading-per-topic, and preserve exact headings only when the user actually requested them.
658	- **Metadata uncertainty.** Source type, publication date, language, or jurisdiction can be missing or wrong. `UNKNOWN` is not evidence of hard compliance. Query a better source, retain the item only for non-binding context where allowed, and disclose uncertainty.
659	- **Compliance-judge circularity.** A generator judging its own prose can over-credit semantic coverage. Prefer deterministic checks for counts, headings, length, table fields, citation resolution, and prohibited content; use a separately configured open-weight judge for semantic coverage with term/output spans, calibrate against human clause audits, and never let it alter faithfulness verdicts.
660	- **Format transformations after verification.** Tables, figures, summaries, citation-style conversion, or truncation can introduce or detach claims. Render from verified structured claims/evidence IDs, re-run citation resolution, and do not use an unconstrained factual model after `strict_verify`.
661	- **Unsatisfiable contracts.** Evidence may not exist under a valid hard scope, or explicit rules may conflict. Bounded scoped search ends in an auditable `UNSATISFIABLE`/`UNKNOWN` result and limitation, never fabricated compliance or relaxed faithfulness.
662	- **Plan drift and stale checkpoints.** Hash every artifact and projection; embed hashes in retrieval, outline, report, and audit metadata. A user amendment supersedes the artifact and invalidates affected checkpoints rather than partially mutating state.
663	- **Interactive over-asking.** Measure question rate, questions per request, edit rate, answer-induced plan changes, abandonment, and downstream regret. Enforce the zero-to-three cap in code, not prompt prose.
664	- **Autonomous blocking.** A benchmark test must run every prompt with no input channel and assert zero `needs_input` exits. Compiler degradation and ambiguity use conservative fallbacks, not a wait state.
665	- **Latency/cost.** Measure gate wall time and tokens separately. Cache deterministic extraction and the canonical artifact by prompt/policy hash. Keep the two-call structure bounded and parallelize safe deterministic work; do not shrink reasoning/output caps.
666	- **Frozen faithfulness regression.** Require a clean diff for `provenance_generator.py`, run existing provenance/strict verification suites, and add path tests proving every recompose route still invokes the unchanged verifier.
667	
668	Implementation spikes and behavioral acceptance checks:
669	
670	- **Schema/compiler spike:** compile all 100 DRB-II prompts in autonomous mode. Human-audit every clause for a stratified set spanning narrow, compound, Chinese, English, source-restricted, time-sensitive, legal, health, finance, and format-heavy prompts. Acceptance is no invented hard constraint, no missing explicit clause, and an assumption record for every inferred/default termвҖ”not merely schema-valid JSON.
671	- **Rule-reader consolidation spike:** port `round1-if-compiler`вҖҷs validated extractor and tests, compare its candidates with `extract_user_constraints`, `extract_instruction_slots`, and `extract_scope_constraints`, and produce a reconciliation corpus. Do not port compose-time eligibility filtering or the coverage-to-heading mapping.
672	- **FS no-starvation spike:** on fresh retrieval for DRB-II 72, prove in telemetry that English/journal constraints and every mandatory topic reach `plan_fs_researcher_queries`/`_plan_expert_facet_queries` before results exist. Compare coverage against the unscoped baseline, but do not substitute source counts for a clause-by-clause evidence audit.
673	- **Compound-plan spike:** use DRB-II 76 and assert every folded requirement has a thread, a mandatory query intent, a section owner, and a final audit record. Force the query budget below mandatory-lane count and verify expand/fail behavior instead of truncation.
674	- **Outline-seam spike:** replay banked corpora for tasks such as 30, 61, 76, and 90 through `refine_outline_from_seed`. Acceptance is actual agentic refinement, preservation of term mappings/locks, scoped gap queries, and no bypass into a second fresh planner.
675	- **Render spike:** exercise an exact section order, comparison table, mind map/timeline, explicit length range, and alternate citation presentation. Every factual cell/node must trace to verified claims; unsupported visual elements must fail visibly.
676	- **Compliance calibration spike:** build human term-level labels for the stratified prompts and compare deterministic plus semantic audit results. Record false passes and false failures by dimension. A report cannot pass globally while a binding term lacks an owning result.
677	- **End-to-end fresh-run spike:** run autonomous fresh retrievalвҶ’outlineвҶ’composeвҶ’render on representative tasks 4, 30, 61, 72, 76, and 90. Audit raw queries, retrieved evidence, outline revisions, verified output, and final contract results line by line. Verify `provenance_generator.py:strict_verify` is unchanged and every factual remediation remains behind it.
678	- **Interactive simulation spike:** script ambiguous prompts with zero, one, and more than three potential questions. Acceptance is no question when broad/open is safe, at most three when material, correct revision after answers/edits, and no retrieval before pinning.
679	
680	The release gate is behavioral: the contract must visibly alter FS discovery, survive outline refinement, constrain composition/rendering, and yield term-level final audit results on real outputs. A diff review, green unit tests, a filtered fixed corpus, or a source-count increase alone does not prove that the Research Planning Gate fired.
681

===gate_flags===
"""Research Planning Gate feature flags (default-OFF).

One tiny module so every stage reads the gate's activation the SAME way and the
OFF path is unambiguously byte-identical. Mirrors the champion flag idiom
(``fs_researcher_enabled`` etc.): a single ``os.getenv`` read, truthy on
``{"1","true","yes","on"}``, default ``"0"`` = OFF.

``PG_GATE`` is the S2 master switch: when unset/0, the retrieval-projection
threading in ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` / ``live_retriever`` / ``run_one_query``
is inert вҖ” every ``retrieval_plan`` argument stays ``None`` and the champion path
runs unchanged. It gates ONLY whether the gate's projection is CONSULTED; it
never itself drops a source or alters faithfulness.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os

_TRUTHY = ("1", "true", "yes", "on")


def gate_enabled() -> bool:
    """True iff the planning-gate retrieval projection is wired ON (default OFF)."""
    return os.getenv("PG_GATE", "0").strip().lower() in _TRUTHY
===__init__===
"""Field-agnostic research-planning package (I-meta-005 Phase 1, #985).

Houses the question-shaped research planner that вҖ” behind the
`PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER` flag вҖ” replaces the clinical-only PICO + clause-split
+ `_ALLOWED_SECTIONS` decomposition path with a field-invariant frame,
faceted sub-queries, and an archetype-tagged section outline.

This is a SHADOW build: nothing here runs unless the on-flag is set AND the
caller explicitly threads the plan through. OFF behavior is byte-identical to
the legacy path. The single Writer call is an injected callable вҖ” the package
NEVER constructs an `OpenRouterClient` or a live HTTP client, so build + smoke
are spend-free.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import (
    DEFAULT_MAX_SUBQUERIES,
    MIN_SUBQUERIES,
    PlannerError,
    ResearchFrame,
    ResearchPlan,
    SectionOutlineItem,
    plan_research,
    serialize_plan_canonical,

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Score a composed POLARIS report with RACE (DeepResearch Bench) against a benchmark task.

RACE (third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py) is REFERENCE-BASED: it matches
the target article, the reference article, and the per-task criteria by EXACT prompt string, then
an LLM judge (openai/gpt-5.5 via OpenRouter) scores both target and reference on the task's
dynamic-weighted dimensions. Overall = target/(target+reference) (0.5 == parity with reference).

This wrapper: (1) reads a report.md, (2) writes it as the target jsonl under the benchmark task's
EXACT prompt (so ref+criteria align), (3) invokes the official RACE harness limited to that task,
(4) prints results/race/<model>/race_result.txt.

Usage:
    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
    python scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/live_compose/run2/report.md \
        --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
DRB = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench"


def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True, help="path to report.md")
    ap.add_argument("--task-id", default="72")
    ap.add_argument("--model-name", default="polaris_task72")
    ap.add_argument("--race-model", default=os.getenv("RACE_MODEL", "openai/gpt-5.5"))
    ap.add_argument("--max-workers", type=int, default=4)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
        print("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set вҖ” source .env first", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    report_text = Path(args.report).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    # Find the benchmark task's EXACT prompt (target/ref/criteria all key on it).
    task = None
    for line in (DRB / "data/prompt_data/query.jsonl").read_text().splitlines():
        o = json.loads(line)
        if str(o["id"]) == str(args.task_id):
            task = o
            break
    if task is None:
        print(f"BLOCKED: task id {args.task_id} not in query.jsonl", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    prompt = task["prompt"]
    lang = task.get("language", "en")

    # Single-task query file so ONLY this task is scored.
    q_path = DRB / f"data/prompt_data/query_task{args.task_id}.jsonl"
    q_path.write_text(json.dumps(task, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 вҖ” DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scorer (re-implementation).

DeepTRACE (Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499, ICLR 2026) defines 8 audit metrics over a report's
statements x sources. There is NO public official scorer/leaderboard, so this is a RE-IMPLEMENTATION whose
output is an ESTIMATE, not a proven score: the paper's judge is GPT-5 (Pearson 0.62 vs human); we substitute
our D8 kimi-k2.6 judge to build the support matrix (DISCLOSED). Calibrate against the paper's published
GPT-5-Deep-Research column before claiming a rank.

Inputs (built upstream from the rendered report):
  * citation_matrix  C : list[list[int]] of shape [n_statements][n_sources]; C[i][j]=1 iff statement i
    cites source j.
  * support_matrix   S : list[list[int]] of the same shape; S[i][j]=1 iff source j's content SUPPORTS
    statement i (per the judge).
  * per-statement metadata: relevant (bool), confidence (int 1-5 or None), stance ('pro'|'con'|'neutral').

Exact formulas (paper section on metrics):
  III Relevant Statements   = relevant / total
  IV  Uncited Sources       = cited / listed           (we also report uncited = 1 - cited/listed)
  V   Unsupported Statements = unsupported / relevant   (a relevant statement with no supporting source)
  VII Citation Accuracy     = sum(C (x) S) / sum(C)
  VIII Citation Thoroughness = sum(C (x) S) / sum(S)
  I   One-Sided  (debate)   = 1 iff NOT (>=1 pro AND >=1 con) relevant statement

I now have a complete picture of the pipeline. The key insight: there's no single fresh-e2e driver that runs gate вҶ’ fresh retrieval вҶ’ outline вҶ’ compose вҶ’ render вҶ’ audit for a single DRB task. The sweep runs on its internal 8-query slate (slugs), and the compose script runs on a frozen corpus. The S5 harness must:

1. Run the FULL gate autonomously (`run_research_planning_gate`) and write `planning_gate_artifact.json`.
2. Run fresh retrieval (via the FS-Researcher path with `PG_GATE=1`).
3. Run outline вҶ’ compose вҶ’ render вҶ’ compliance audit, producing `report.md`, `contract_compliance.json`, telemetry.

===drbii_wrapper===
"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 part 2 вҖ” DeepResearch-Bench-II (DRB-II) submission WRAPPER (thin, offline).

DRB-II is a REAL, official research-report benchmark (132 tasks / 9,415 expert rubric items, Gemini
rubric judge). It ships its own scorer, ``third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py``, which:
  * scans a ``--pdf_dir`` tree for ``<model>/idx-<N>.<ext>`` files (``.md``/``.txt``/``.html``/``.pdf``/
    ``.docx``/images),
  * reads each file's ARTICLE text as the ``<passage>`` to grade,
  * pulls the task text + rubric items + ``blocked`` reference from the catalog
    ``tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl`` keyed by the file's ``idx`` (NOT from the submission),
  * asks the Gemini judge for a THREE-WAY (1 / 0 / -1) score per rubric item,
  * appends per-idx results to ``--out_jsonl``.

This module is a THIN ADAPTER only. It does NOT re-implement DRB-II scoring вҖ” the official harness stays
the scorer (contrast the DeepTRACE module, which is a re-implementation because DeepTRACE has NO public
scorer). Two responsibilities, both PURE / OFFLINE:

  1. ``build_drbii_submission(report_markdown, task_id, references)`` -> the DRB-II-shaped submission dict
     (article text + DRB-II task idx + normalized reference list + the on-disk ``idx-<N>.md`` filename).
  2. ``drbii_score_command(submission_path, out_path)`` -> the argv that invokes ``run_evaluation.py`` at
     benchmark time. The harness path is parameterized by ``PG_DRBII_EVAL_SCRIPT`` (default: a documented
     placeholder path). The wrapper NEVER imports or requires the harness / Gemini / network at
     import/test time вҖ” the actual scoring runs later on the VM.

``write_drbii_submission`` is a convenience materializer that writes the dict into the exact on-disk layout
the harness scans. It is a plain file write (offline).

Honesty / faithfulness notes:
  * task_id IS the DRB-II ``idx``. The run-id <-> idx offset is REAL (run id "task72" is DRB-II idx 56).
    This wrapper does NOT transform ids вҖ” the caller passes the resolved DRB-II ``idx``. A wrong idx would
    score the report against the wrong task's rubrics (the В§-1.4 silently-wrong-number class); build-time
    validation guards only the shape, not the id-resolution (that lives in ``pack_drb2.py``).
  * ``references`` are OUR article's cited sources, captured for provenance and so a downstream check can
    compare them against the task's ``blocked`` reference. They are NOT the ``blocked`` field (which the
    harness reads from its own catalog).
  * No kill-switch: this is a NEW standalone eval-tooling module with ZERO import into the runtime
    pipeline, so it changes no existing pipeline behavior. The faithfulness engine is untouched.
  * LAW VI: every path/name is env-overridable; nothing is hard-coded into behavior.
"""

from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional, Sequence

# wrapper stays offline-safe (no filesystem/import dependency) at import/test time.

I now have complete understanding of the entry points. `run_gate_b_query(q, out_root, ...)` is the sanctioned fresh single-task retrieval+compose+render entry that internally reaches `run_one_query` (which has the S2 gate hook and produces `report.md`, protocol, corpus, etc.). 

The S5 harness must:
1. **Stage GATE**: run `run_research_planning_gate(prompt, mode="autonomous")` (with `PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1`), write `planning_gate_artifact.json`.
2. **Stage FRESH E2E**: set `PG_GATE=1` + the full-capability slate, build a query dict from the DRB task prompt, call `run_gate_b_query(q, out_root)` вҶ’ fresh retrieval вҶ’ outline FEED вҶ’ compose вҶ’ render вҶ’ `report.md`.
3. **Stage AUDIT**: run `audit_contract` producing `contract_compliance.json`.
4. **Scoring**: RACE via `score_report_race.py`, FACT via the FACT/scorecard pipeline. N draws.
5. **--dry / --plan-only**: exercises gate + planning + wiring assembly WITHOUT live retrieval/compose.

"""S1 SMOKE audit вҖ” compile stratified DRB prompts through the Research Planning
Gate in AUTONOMOUS mode and assert the two S1 acceptance properties:

  1. NO invented hard constraint  вҖ” every ``force==hard`` term in the compiled
     contract carries origin in {explicit, user_answer, user_edit} AND a
     quote-verified prompt span (validate_contract returns clean).
  2. An ASSUMPTION record for every inferred term вҖ” every inferred /
     policy_default term that carries a value is disclosed in the contract's
     ``assumptions`` (the autonomous sweep backfills any the compiler omitted).

It also asserts the load-bearing autonomous invariant (``needs_input`` is always
False; state is ``auto_pinned`` or ``unsatisfiable``).

Two run modes
-------------
* **OFFLINE (default, spend-free, in-workflow safe).** A deterministic compiler
  STUB derives the contract from the REAL S0 candidate adapter output (promoting
  only span-verified hard candidates; everything else preference/open; disclosing
  inferred terms). This exercises the whole gate machinery вҖ” candidate seeding вҶ’
  contract parse вҶ’ autonomous disclosure вҶ’ deterministic validation вҶ’ hashing вҖ”
  without a network call, so it runs inside the 10-min workflow cap.

* **LIVE (monitored job only).** ``--live`` drops the stub and lets the gate call
  the real small policy model (``PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1``). This is the FULL
  100-prompt audit path; it is DEFERRED to a monitored job (do not run it in the
  capped workflow). This script is the documented harness for it.

Usage
-----
    python3 scripts/dr_benchmark/gate_s1_smoke.py            # offline, 8 prompts
    python3 scripts/dr_benchmark/gate_s1_smoke.py --all      # offline, 100 prompts
    PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1 python3 scripts/dr_benchmark/gate_s1_smoke.py --live --all
